title: Aster shimadae
taxonomy: Aster shimadae is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a type of daisy that is native to Japan. The classification of Aster shimadae is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Aster, Species: A. shimadae. Within the genus Aster, Aster shimadae is a distinct species.

intro: Aster shimadae, also known as Shimada's aster, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Aster shimadae include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aster shimadae is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into skincare products due to its purported anti-aging and wound-healing effects.
